title: Composite materials
abstract: The present disclosure relates to composite materials comprising a recycled polymer material and a waste glass fiber material, and methods of manufacturing such composite materials. The described materials have been found to show a number of good mechanical properties.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A composite material comprising: at least one layer of a waste glass fiber mat laminated to at least one layer of a recycled polymer material; wherein the recycled polymer material has a density lower than about 1 g/cm 3 . 2. A composite material according to claim 1 , wherein the pore size in the waste glass fiber mat is between about 5 pm to about 50 pm. 3. A composite material according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the waste glass fiber mat comprises staple glass fibers having a nominal length of between about 5 mm to about 100 mm. 4. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the waste glass fiber mat comprises staple glass fibers having diameters in the range of between about 1 pm to 50 pm. 5. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the recycled polymer material is a mixed waste polymer material, and/or comprises at least two or more different polymers. 6. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the recycled polymer material comprises a mixture of two or more thermoplastic polymers, optionally selected from polyethylene, and polypropylene. 7. A composite material according to claim 5 or 6, wherein the two or more different polymers are each independently selected from high density polyethylene (HDPE), low density polyethylene (LDPE), linear low density polyethylene (LLDPE), medium density polyethylene (MDPE), chlorinated polyethylene (CPE), very low density polyethylene (VLDPE), high molecular weight polyethylene (HMWPE), ultra-low-molecular weight polyethylene (ULMWPE) and ultra-high-molecular weight polyethylene (UHMWPE), polypropylene (PP), and combinations thereof. 8. A composite material according to claim 6 or 7, wherein the recycled polymer material comprises multiple adjacent bonded areas of the two or more thermoplastic polymers, each area having a size of between 1 mm to 200 mm, preferably a size of between 5 mm to 50 mm in one direction. 9. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein: (i) the recycled polymer material comprises or is a post-consumer or postindustrial mixed plastic waste material; and/or (ii) the waste glass fiber mat comprises or is a post-consumer or post-industrial waste glass fiber mat. 10. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one layer of recycled polymer material is a film or sheet formed by pressing (e.g. compression molding) shredded polymer material under heat and pressure, optionally shredded waste polymer films or sheets. 11. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the composite material comprises a plurality of layers of the recycled polymer material and/or a plurality of layers of the waste glass fiber mat, and optionally wherein the number of waste glass fiber mat layers is between 5 to 20 times higher than the number of recycled polymer material layers. 12. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein at least one layer of recycled polymer material is sandwiched between a first and second layer of the waste glass fiber mat. 13. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein at least one layer of recycled polymer material is sandwiched between a first section or block comprising a plurality of layers of the waste glass fiber mat and a second section or block comprising a plurality of layers of the waste glass fiber mat. 14. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the composite material comprises the waste glass fiber mat in an amount between about 5% and about 50% by volume, between about 15% and 40% by volume, or between about 20% and 35% by volume, based on the total volume of composite material. 15. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the glass fiber fraction is between about 5% and about 70%, between about 10% and about 60%, or between about 15% and 50% by weight, based on the total weight of the composite material. 16. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein a void content in the composite material is less than about 20%, or less than about 15% based on the total volume of the composite material. 17. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein a void content in the composite material is between about 1 % and about 20%, between about 5% and about 15%, or between about 7% and about 13% based on the total volume of the composite material. 18. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the waste glass fiber mat comprises between about 5% and about 50%, between about 10% and about 30%, or between about 15% and about 25% by weight of a binder based on the total weight of the waste glass fiber mat, optionally wherein the binder is selected from acrylic and/or melamine formaldehyde and/or urea formaldehyde containing binder compositions. 19. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one layer of the recycled polymer material has a thickness between about 0.1 mm and about 20mm, or between about 0.25 mm and 1 ,5mm. 20. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one layer of waste glass fiber mat has a mean area weight of between 30 g/m 2 up to 300 g/m 2 and a corresponding thickness between about 0.1 mm and about 3mm, preferably about 0.3mm and about 1mm, such as between about 0.4mm and about 1 mm. 21 . A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the density of the composite material is at least about 0.25 g/cm 3 , at least about 0.50 g/cm 3 -, 

at least about 0.75 g/cm 3 , or at least about 1 .0 g/cm 3 or between about 0.50g/cm 3 and about 2.0 g/cm 3 , such as between about 1 .0 g/cm 3 and 1 .5 g/cm 3 . 22. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the tensile strength is at least about 5 MPa, at least 10 MPa, at least about 15 MPa, at least about 20 MPa, at least about 30 MPa, or between about 25 MPa and about 50 MPa. 23. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the flexural modulus is at least 0.5 GPa, at least about 1 GPa, at least about 3 GPa, at least about 5 GPa, or between about 0.5 GPa and 20 GPa, such as between about 1 GPa and 10 GPa. 24. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, comprising one or more (or all) of the following: (i) a tensile modulus of at least about 1 GPa, at least about 1.5 GPa, at least about 2 GPa, between about 1 GPa and about 10 GPa, between about 1 GPa and about 8 GPa, or between about 1 GPa and about 4 GPa; (ii) a flexural strength of at least about 10 MPa, at least about 20 MPa, or at least about 30 MPa; (iii) a compressive strength of at least about 10 MPa, at least about 15 MPa, at least about 20 MPa, at least about 25 MPa, or between about 10 MPa and 40 MPa; and/or (iv) a compressive modulus of at least about 1 GPa, at least about 1.5 GPa, at least about 2.0 GPa, between about 1 GPa and 7 GPa, or between about 1.5 GPa and 6 GPa. 25. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the recycled polymer material is present in an amount between about 10% and about 90%, between about 20% and about 80%, preferably between about 25% and about 80%, or between about 50% and about 75% by weight based on the total weight of the composite. 26. A composite material according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the composite material comprises at least about 15% by volume of the glass fiber, based on the total volume of the composite material, and a tensile modulus of at least about 2 GPa or at least about 3 GPa. 27. A composite laminate according to any one of the preceding claims, comprising one or more, or all, of the following properties: (i) between about 20% and about 35% by volume of glass fibers based on the total volume of the composite material; (ii) a void between about 5% and about 15%, or between about 7% and about 13% based on the total volume of the composite material; and (ii) a density between about 1.0 g/cm 3 and 1.5 g/cm 3 . 28. A method for making a composite material comprising: (a) laminating at least one layer of a recycled polymer material to at least one layer of waste glass fiber mat; wherein the recycled polymer material has a density lower than about 1 g/cm 3 . 29. The method according to claim 28, wherein: (i) the recycled polymer material is a mixed waste polymer material; 30. The method according to claim 28 or 29, further comprising: contacting waste polymer material with water; and then separating the polymer material that floats; wherein the separated polymer material is the recycled polymer material. 31 . The method of any one of claims 28 to 30, further comprising: (a) shredding a recycled polymer material to provide a shredded recycled polymer material; and (b) pressing the shredded recycled polymer material to provide a sheet or film of recycled polymer material; wherein the sheet or film of mixed polymer material provides the at least one layer of recycled polymer material. 32. The method of any one of claims 28 to 31 , further comprising: (a) providing a layer of waste glass fiber mat; and (b) coating the layer of waste glass fiber mat with a layer of mixed waste polymer material to provide a layer of waste glass fiber mat coated with the mixed waste polymer material. 33. The method of any one of claims 28 to 32, wherein the composite laminate comprises a plurality of layers of the recycled polymer material and/or a plurality of layers of the waste glass fiber mat, the method further comprising: (a) stacking the plurality of layers of recycled polymer material and the plurality of layers of waste glass fiber mat in a desired arrangement or lay-up; and (b) laminating the layers together. 34. The method of any one of claims 28 to 33, further comprising: sandwiching at least one layer of recycled polymer material between a first and second layer of waste glass fiber mat and laminating the layers together. 35. The method of any one of claims 28 to 34, further comprising: (a) preparing one or more construct(s) prior to the lamination step, each construct comprising a layer of waste glass fiber mat bonded to a layer of recycled polymer material; and (b) laminating the one or more constructs to one or more additional layers of waste glass fiber mats to provide the composite material. 36. The method of any one of claims 28 to 35, wherein the laminating step comprises bonding the layers together by applying a temperature in between 150 Â°C and 200 Â°C and pressure in between 10 bar and 30 bar such that the recycled polymer material melts and binds to the at least one layers or plurality of layers of waste glass fiber mat, bonding the layers together. 37. The method of any one of claims 28 to 36, wherein the composite material is as defined in any one of claims 1 to 27. 38. Use of a composite material as defined in any one of claims 1 to 27, or made in accordance with the method of any one of claims 28 to 37, in the construction sector, in the transport sector, in the sports sector, and/or in the agricultural sector.
